title: RUN-4539: Change podman module paths
body:
The podman module paths are moving from github.com/containers/podman to go.podman.io/podman.  This will help with future mobility.

<!--

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

comments.body:
> Try `make lint ; ./bin/golangci-lint fmt`.

yeah i do run that but this is a windows failure.
tmt tests failed for commit 7f50824c1219145765c1b5d8079c06bbded94660. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.
tmt tests failed for commit 84409a9131aaec716bc9355ade6c8c5a5eae9de2. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.

> I think you need hit up the podman.spec as well, i.e. check ld_project

yup. That would have to change, but dunno if that'd cause breakage in fetching the source tarball. Doesn't seem to be critical to this PR AFAICT. I can take that in a followup, unless @baude wants to do it here itself.

> > I think you need hit up the podman.spec as well, i.e. check ld_project
> 
> yup. That would have to change, but dunno if that'd cause breakage in fetching the source tarball. Doesn't seem to be critical to this PR AFAICT. I can take that in a followup, unless @baude wants to do it here itself.

Well they make the tmt tests fail as the timestamp is wrong due the incorrect linker flag. I mean tmt is not blocking so sure we could go ahead but then at the cost of following PRs also all turning tmt red.

```
From ae92aa5f5ae96a2ee17ffb00f46e8a7db4390b37 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@redhat.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Apr 2026 14:00:42 -0400
Subject: [PATCH] rpm: update gopaths to account for vanity import

Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@redhat.com>
---
 rpm/podman.spec | 8 ++------
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)

diff --git a/rpm/podman.spec b/rpm/podman.spec
index 160bcb02de..ee05a1c2af 100644
--- a/rpm/podman.spec
+++ b/rpm/podman.spec
@@ -32,16 +32,12 @@
 %define fips_enabled 1
 %endif

-%global container_base_path github.com/containers
-%global container_base_url https://%{container_base_path}
+%global container_base_path go.podman.io

 # For LDFLAGS
 %global ld_project %{container_base_path}/%{name}/v6
 %global ld_libpod %{ld_project}/libpod

-# %%{name}
-%global git0 %{container_base_url}/%{name}
-
 # podman-machine subpackage will be present only on these architectures
 %global machine_arches x86_64 aarch64

@@ -75,7 +71,7 @@ ExclusiveArch: aarch64 ppc64le s390x x86_64 riscv64
 Summary: Manage Pods, Containers and Container Images
 URL: https://%{name}.io/
 # All SourceN files fetched from upstream
-Source0: %{git0}/archive/v%{version_no_tilde}.tar.gz
+Source0: https://github.com/containers/%{name}/archive/v%{version_no_tilde}.tar.gz
 Provides: %{name}-manpages = %{epoch}:%{version}-%{release}
 BuildRequires: %{_bindir}/envsubst
 %if %{defined build_with_btrfs}
-- 
2.54.0

```

@baude this patch should cleanly apply on your current PR branch in case you add it here. My extent of testing: `packit build locally` worked fine .
Tried the binary built via `packit build locally` on local machine and the podman version test passed. So, I'm quite positive that patch will work if you wanna add it here itself.
tmt tests failed for commit 2cc3be73323e9dfbeaa9d603cac7f359bb8b8f05. @lsm5, @psss, @thrix please check.
